imageDataDeliveryEnabled.description: Set to include image data in the log delivery.

embeddingDataDeliveryEnabled.description: Set to include embeddings data in the log delivery.

videoDataDeliveryEnabled.description: Set to include video data in the log delivery.

nextToken.description: The pagination token to use in a subsequent request to retrieve the next page of results.

modelDeploymentSummaries.description: A list of custom model deployment summaries.

customModelDeploymentArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the custom model deployment.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the custom model deployment was last modified.

failureMessage.description: If the deployment status is FAILED, this field contains a message describing the failure reason.

nextToken.description: If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.

customizationType.description: Specifies whether to carry out continued pre-training of a model or whether to fine-tune it. For more information, see Custom models.

ownerAccountId.description: The unique identifier of the account that owns the model.

modelStatus.description:
The current status of the custom model. Possible values include:

  • Creating - The model is being created and validated.

  • Active - The model has been successfully created and is ready for use.

  • Failed - The model creation process failed.

nextToken.description: Opaque continuation token of previous paginated response.

configId.description: Unique ID for the account enforced configuration.

inputTags.description: Whether to honor or ignore input tags at runtime.

createdAt.description: Timestamp.

createdBy.description: The ARN of the role used to update the configuration.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp.

updatedBy.description: The ARN of the role used to update the configuration.

nextToken.description: Continuation token from the previous response, for Amazon Bedrock to list the next set of results.

status.description: The current status of the evaluation job.

creationTime.description: The time the evaluation job was created.

jobType.description: Specifies whether the evaluation job is automated or human-based.

evaluationTaskTypes.description: The type of task for model evaluation.

inferenceConfigSummary.description: Identifies the models, Knowledge Bases, or other RAG sources evaluated in a model or Knowledge Base evaluation job.

modelConfigSummary.description: A summary of the models used in an Amazon Bedrock model evaluation job. These resources can be models in Amazon Bedrock or models outside of Amazon Bedrock that you use to generate your own inference response data.

precomputedInferenceSourceIdentifiers.description: A label that identifies the models used for a model evaluation job where you provide your own inference response data.

ragConfigSummary.description: A summary of the RAG resources used in an Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Base evaluation job. These resources can be Knowledge Bases in Amazon Bedrock or RAG sources outside of Amazon Bedrock that you use to generate your own inference response data.

bedrockKnowledgeBaseIdentifiers.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of the Knowledge Base resources used for a Knowledge Base evaluation job where Amazon Bedrock invokes the Knowledge Base for you.

precomputedRagSourceIdentifiers.description: A label that identifies the RAG sources used for a Knowledge Base evaluation job where you provide your own inference response data.

applicationType.description: Specifies whether the evaluation job is for evaluating a model or evaluating a knowledge base (retrieval and response generation).

inputModalities.description: The input modalities that the model supports.

outputModalities.description: The output modalities that the model supports.

inferenceTypesSupported.description: The inference types that the model supports.

modelLifecycle.description: Contains details about whether a model version is available or deprecated.

status.description: Specifies whether a model version is available (ACTIVE) or deprecated (LEGACY.

guardrails.description: A list of objects, each of which contains details about a guardrail.

nextToken.description: If there are more results than were returned in the response, the response returns a nextToken that you can send in another ListGuardrails request to see the next batch of results.

status.description: The status of the guardrail.

createdAt.description: The date and time at which the guardrail was created.

updatedAt.description: The date and time at which the guardrail was last updated.

crossRegionDetails.description: Details about the system-defined guardrail profile that you're using with your guardrail, including the guardrail profile ID and Amazon Resource Name (ARN).

guardrailProfileId.description: The ID of the guardrail profile that your guardrail is using. Profile availability depends on your current Amazon Web Services Region. For more information, see the Amazon Bedrock User Guide.

guardrailProfileArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the guardrail profile that you're using with your guardrail.

nextToken.description: If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.

modelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the imported model.

instructSupported.description: Specifies if the imported model supports converse.

inferenceProfileSummaries.description: A list of information about each inference profile that you can use.

nextToken.description: If the total number of results is greater than the maxResults value provided in the request, use this token when making another request in the nextToken field to return the next batch of results.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the inference profile was last updated.

inferenceProfileArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the inference profile.

models.description: A list of information about each model in the inference profile.

inferenceProfileId.description: The unique identifier of the inference profile.

status.description: The status of the inference profile. ACTIVE means that the inference profile is ready to be used.

type.description:
The type of the inference profile. The following types are possible:

  • SYSTEM_DEFINED – The inference profile is defined by Amazon Bedrock. You can route inference requests across regions with these inference profiles.

  • APPLICATION – The inference profile was created by a user. This type of inference profile can track metrics and costs when invoking the model in it. The inference profile may route requests to one or multiple regions.

nextToken.description: The token for the next set of results. Use this token to get the next set of results.

modelSourceIdentifier.description: The ARN of the model from Amazon Bedrock Marketplace that is deployed on this endpoint.

statusMessage.description: Additional information about the overall status, if available.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the endpoint was last updated.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resoource Name (ARN) of the model copy job.

lastModifiedTime.description: The time when the import job was last modified.

invocationJobSummaries.description: A list of items, each of which contains a summary about a batch inference job.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the batch inference job.

jobName.description: The name of the batch inference job.

modelId.description: The unique identifier of the foundation model used for model inference.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, case-sensitive identifier to ensure that the API request completes no more than one time. If this token matches a previous request, Amazon Bedrock ignores the request, but does not return an error. For more information, see Ensuring idempotency.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the service role with permissions to carry out and manage batch inference. You can use the console to create a default service role or follow the steps at Create a service role for batch inference.

status.description:
The status of the batch inference job.

The following statuses are possible:

  • Submitted – This job has been submitted to a queue for validation.

  • Validating – This job is being validated for the requirements described in Format and upload your batch inference data. The criteria include the following:

    • Your IAM service role has access to the Amazon S3 buckets containing your files.

    • Your files are .jsonl files and each individual record is a JSON object in the correct format. Note that validation doesn't check if the modelInput value matches the request body for the model.

    • Your files fulfill the requirements for file size and number of records. For more information, see Quotas for Amazon Bedrock.

  • Scheduled – This job has been validated and is now in a queue. The job will automatically start when it reaches its turn.

  • Expired – This job timed out because it was scheduled but didn't begin before the set timeout duration. Submit a new job request.

  • InProgress – This job has begun. You can start viewing the results in the output S3 location.

  • Completed – This job has successfully completed. View the output files in the output S3 location.

  • PartiallyCompleted – This job has partially completed. Not all of your records could be processed in time. View the output files in the output S3 location.

  • Failed – This job has failed. Check the failure message for any further details. For further assistance, reach out to the Amazon Web Services Support Center.

  • Stopped – This job was stopped by a user.

  • Stopping – This job is being stopped by a user.

message.description: If the batch inference job failed, this field contains a message describing why the job failed.

submitTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job was submitted.

lastModifiedTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job was last modified.

endTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job ended.

inputDataConfig.description: Details about the location of the input to the batch inference job.

s3InputDataConfig.description: Contains the configuration of the S3 location of the input data.

s3Uri.description: The S3 location of the input data.

s3BucketOwner.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account that owns the S3 bucket containing the input data.

outputDataConfig.description: Details about the location of the output of the batch inference job.

s3OutputDataConfig.description: Contains the configuration of the S3 location of the output data.

s3Uri.description: The S3 location of the output data.

s3EncryptionKeyId.description: The unique identifier of the key that encrypts the S3 location of the output data.

s3BucketOwner.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account that owns the S3 bucket containing the output data.

vpcConfig.description: The configuration of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) for the data in the batch inference job. For more information, see Protect batch inference jobs using a VPC.

subnetIds.description: An array of IDs for each subnet in the VPC to use.

securityGroupIds.description: An array of IDs for each security group in the VPC to use.

timeoutDurationInHours.description: The number of hours after which the batch inference job was set to time out.

jobExpirationTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job times or timed out.

nextToken.description: Specify the pagination token from a previous request to retrieve the next page of results.

updatedAt.description: When the router was updated.

status.description: The router's status.

provisionedModelSummaries.description: A list of summaries, one for each Provisioned Throughput in the response.

provisionedModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Provisioned Throughput.

modelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the model associated with the Provisioned Throughput.

desiredModelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the model requested to be associated to this Provisioned Throughput. This value differs from the modelArn if updating hasn't completed.

modelUnits.description: The number of model units allocated to the Provisioned Throughput.

desiredModelUnits.description: The number of model units that was requested to be allocated to the Provisioned Throughput.

status.description: The status of the Provisioned Throughput.

commitmentExpirationTime.description: The timestamp for when the commitment term of the Provisioned Throughput expires.

creationTime.description: The time that the Provisioned Throughput was created.

lastModifiedTime.description: The time that the Provisioned Throughput was last modified.

jobIdentifier.description: The ARN of the evaluation job being deleted.

code.description: A HTTP status code of the evaluation job being deleted.

message.description: A status message about the evaluation job deletion.

version.description: The version number of the newly created Automated Reasoning policy. The initial version is always DRAFT.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the evaluation job.

modelId.description: Model Id of the model for the access request.

version.description: The number of the version of the guardrail.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the endpoint was last updated.

initialInstanceCount.description: The number of Amazon EC2 compute instances to deploy for initial endpoint creation.

instanceType.description: The Amazon EC2 compute instance type to deploy for hosting the model.

executionRole.description: The ARN of the IAM role that Amazon SageMaker can assume to access model artifacts and docker image for deployment on Amazon EC2 compute instances or for batch transform jobs.

kmsEncryptionKey.description: The Amazon Web Services KMS key that Amazon SageMaker uses to encrypt data on the storage volume attached to the Amazon EC2 compute instance that hosts the endpoint.

policyDefinition.description: The exported policy definition containing the formal logic rules, variables, and custom variable types.

version.description: The version of the policy definition format.

types.description: The custom user-defined vairable types used in the policy. Types are enum-based variable types that provide additional context beyond the predefined variable types.

rules.description: The formal logic rules extracted from the source document. Rules define the logical constraints that determine whether model responses are valid, invalid, or satisfiable.

variables.description: The variables that represent concepts in the policy. Variables can have values assigned when translating natural language into formal logic. Their descriptions are crucial for accurate translation.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the policy.

definitionHash.description: The hash of the policy definition used as a concurrency token.

kmsKeyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the KMS key used to encrypt the automated reasoning policy and its associated artifacts. If a KMS key is not provided during the initial CreateAutomatedReasoningPolicyRequest, the kmsKeyArn won't be included in the GetAutomatedReasoningPolicyResponse.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the policy was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the policy was last updated.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

name.description: The name of the Automated Reasoning policy.

buildWorkflowId.description: The unique identifier of the build workflow.

annotations.description: The current set of annotations containing rules, variables, and types extracted from the source documents. These can be modified before finalizing the policy.

annotationSetHash.description: A hash value representing the current state of the annotations. This is used for optimistic concurrency control when updating annotations.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the annotations were last updated.

addType.description: An operation to add a new custom type to the policy, defining a set of possible values for policy variables.

name.description: The name of the new custom type. This name will be used to reference the type in variable definitions and rules.

description.description: A description of what the custom type represents and how it should be used in the policy.

values.description: The list of possible values that variables of this type can take, each with its own description and identifier.

updateType.description: An operation to modify an existing custom type in the policy, such as changing its name, description, or allowed values.

name.description: The current name of the custom type to update.

newName.description: The new name for the custom type, if you want to rename it. If not provided, the name remains unchanged.

description.description: The new description for the custom type, replacing the previous description.

values.description: The updated list of values for the custom type, which can include additions, modifications, or removals.

deleteType.description: An operation to remove a custom type from the policy. The type must not be referenced by any variables or rules.

name.description: The name of the custom type to delete from the policy. The type must not be referenced by any variables or rules.

addVariable.description: An operation to add a new variable to the policy, which can be used in rule expressions to represent dynamic values.

name.description: The name of the new variable. This name will be used to reference the variable in rule expressions.

type.description: The type of the variable, which can be a built-in type (like string or number) or a custom type defined in the policy.

description.description: A description of what the variable represents and how it should be used in rules.

updateVariable.description: An operation to modify an existing variable in the policy, such as changing its name, type, or description.

name.description: The current name of the variable to update.

newName.description: The new name for the variable, if you want to rename it. If not provided, the name remains unchanged.

description.description: The new description for the variable, replacing the previous description.

deleteVariable.description: An operation to remove a variable from the policy. The variable must not be referenced by any rules.

name.description: The name of the variable to delete from the policy. The variable must not be referenced by any rules.

addRule.description: An operation to add a new logical rule to the policy using formal mathematical expressions.

expression.description: The formal logical expression that defines the rule, using mathematical notation and referencing policy variables and types.

updateRule.description: An operation to modify an existing rule in the policy, such as changing its logical expression or conditions.

ruleId.description: The unique identifier of the rule to update.

expression.description: The new formal logical expression for the rule, replacing the previous expression.

deleteRule.description: An operation to remove a rule from the policy.

ruleId.description: The unique identifier of the rule to delete from the policy.

addRuleFromNaturalLanguage.description: An operation to add a new rule by converting natural language descriptions into formal logical expressions.

naturalLanguage.description: The natural language description of the rule that should be converted into a formal logical expression.

updateFromRulesFeedback.description: An operation to update the policy based on feedback about how specific rules performed during testing or validation.

ruleIds.description: The list of rule identifiers that the feedback applies to.

feedback.description: The feedback information about rule performance, including suggestions for improvements or corrections.

updateFromScenarioFeedback.description: An operation to update the policy based on feedback about how it performed on specific test scenarios.

ruleIds.description: The list of rule identifiers that were involved in the scenario being evaluated.

scenarioExpression.description: The logical expression that defines the test scenario that generated this feedback.

feedback.description: The feedback information about scenario performance, including any issues or improvements identified.

ingestContent.description: An operation to process and incorporate new content into the policy, extracting additional rules and concepts.

content.description: The new content to be analyzed and incorporated into the policy, such as additional documents or rule descriptions.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

buildWorkflowId.description: The unique identifier of the build workflow.

status.description: The current status of the build workflow (e.g., RUNNING, COMPLETED, FAILED, CANCELLED).

buildWorkflowType.description: The type of build workflow being executed (e.g., DOCUMENT_INGESTION, POLICY_REPAIR).

documentName.description: The name of the source document used in the build workflow.

documentContentType.description: The content type of the source document (e.g., text/plain, application/pdf).

documentDescription.description: A detailed description of the document's content and how it should be used in the policy generation process.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the build workflow was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the build workflow was last updated.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

buildWorkflowId.description: The unique identifier of the build workflow.

buildWorkflowAssets.description: The requested build workflow asset. This is a union type that returns only one of the available asset types (logs, reports, or generated artifacts) based on the specific asset type requested in the API call.

policyDefinition.description: The complete policy definition generated by the build workflow, containing all rules, variables, and custom types extracted from the source documents.

version.description: The version of the policy definition format.

types.description: The custom user-defined vairable types used in the policy. Types are enum-based variable types that provide additional context beyond the predefined variable types.

rules.description: The formal logic rules extracted from the source document. Rules define the logical constraints that determine whether model responses are valid, invalid, or satisfiable.

variables.description: The variables that represent concepts in the policy. Variables can have values assigned when translating natural language into formal logic. Their descriptions are crucial for accurate translation.

qualityReport.description: A comprehensive report analyzing the quality of the generated policy, including metrics about rule coverage, potential conflicts, and unused elements.

typeCount.description: The total number of custom types defined in the policy.

variableCount.description: The total number of variables defined in the policy.

ruleCount.description: The total number of rules defined in the policy.

unusedTypes.description: A list of custom types that are defined but not referenced by any variables or rules, suggesting they may be unnecessary.

unusedTypeValues.description: A list of type values that are defined but never used in any rules, indicating potential cleanup opportunities.

unusedVariables.description: A list of variables that are defined but not referenced by any rules, suggesting they may be unnecessary.

conflictingRules.description: A list of rules that may conflict with each other, potentially leading to inconsistent policy behavior.

disjointRuleSets.description: Groups of rules that operate on completely separate sets of variables, indicating the policy may be addressing multiple unrelated concerns.

buildLog.description: The complete build log containing detailed information about each step in the policy generation process.

entries.description: A list of log entries documenting each step in the policy build process, including timestamps, status, and detailed messages.

generatedTestCases.description: A comprehensive test suite generated by the build workflow, providing validation capabilities for automated reasoning policies.
Represents a collection of generated test cases.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy.

scenario.description: The next test scenario to validate, including the test expression and expected results.

expression.description: The logical expression or condition that defines this test scenario.

alternateExpression.description: An alternative way to express the same test scenario, used for validation and comparison purposes.

ruleIds.description: The list of rule identifiers that are expected to be triggered or evaluated by this test scenario.

expectedResult.description: The expected outcome when this scenario is evaluated against the policy (e.g., PASS, FAIL, VIOLATION).

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the policy that contains the test.

testCase.description: The test details including the content, query, expected result, and metadata.

guardContent.description: The output content to be validated by the policy, typically representing a foundation model response.

queryContent.description: The input query or prompt that generated the content. This provides context for the validation.

expectedAggregatedFindingsResult.description: The expected result of the Automated Reasoning check for this test.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the test was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the test was last updated.

confidenceThreshold.description: The minimum confidence level for logic validation. Content meeting this threshold is considered high-confidence and can be validated.

testResult.description: The test result containing validation findings, execution status, and detailed analysis.

testCase.description: The test case that was executed, including the input content, expected results, and configuration parameters used during validation.

guardContent.description: The output content to be validated by the policy, typically representing a foundation model response.

queryContent.description: The input query or prompt that generated the content. This provides context for the validation.

expectedAggregatedFindingsResult.description: The expected result of the Automated Reasoning check for this test.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the test was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the test was last updated.

confidenceThreshold.description: The minimum confidence level for logic validation. Content meeting this threshold is considered high-confidence and can be validated.

policyArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Automated Reasoning policy that was tested.

testRunStatus.description: The overall status of the test run (e.g., COMPLETED, FAILED, IN_PROGRESS).

testFindings.description: Detailed findings from the test run, including any issues, violations, or unexpected behaviors discovered.

testRunResult.description: The overall result of the test run, indicating whether the policy passed or failed validation.

aggregatedTestFindingsResult.description: A summary of all test findings, aggregated to provide an overall assessment of policy quality and correctness.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the test results were last updated.

modelArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with this model.

jobArn.description: Job Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with this model. For models that you create with the CreateCustomModel API operation, this is NULL.

baseModelArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the base model.

modelKmsKeyArn.description: The custom model is encrypted at rest using this key.

hyperParameters.description: Hyperparameter values associated with this model. For details on the format for different models, see Custom model hyperparameters.

outputDataConfig.description: Output data configuration associated with this custom model.

trainingMetrics.description: Contains training metrics from the job creation.

validationMetrics.description: The validation metrics from the job creation.

customizationConfig.description: The customization configuration for the custom model.

modelStatus.description:
The current status of the custom model. Possible values include:

  • Creating - The model is being created and validated.

  • Active - The model has been successfully created and is ready for use.

  • Failed - The model creation process failed. Check the failureMessage field for details.

failureMessage.description: A failure message for any issues that occurred when creating the custom model. This is included for only a failed CreateCustomModel operation.

validationLoss.description: The validation loss associated with this validator.

customModelDeploymentArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the custom model deployment.

modelDeploymentName.description: The name of the custom model deployment.

modelArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the custom model associated with this deployment.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the custom model deployment was created.

status.description:
The status of the custom model deployment. Possible values are:

  • CREATING - The deployment is being set up and prepared for inference.

  • ACTIVE - The deployment is ready and available for inference requests.

  • FAILED - The deployment failed to be created or became unavailable.

description.description: The description of the custom model deployment.

failureMessage.description: If the deployment status is FAILED, this field contains a message describing the failure reason.

lastUpdatedAt.description: The date and time when the custom model deployment was last updated.

status.description: The current status of the evaluation job.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM service role used in the evaluation job.

jobType.description: Specifies whether the evaluation job is automated or human-based.

applicationType.description: Specifies whether the evaluation job is for evaluating a model or evaluating a knowledge base (retrieval and response generation).

evaluationConfig.description: Contains the configuration details of either an automated or human-based evaluation job.

inferenceConfig.description: Contains the configuration details of the inference model used for the evaluation job.

outputDataConfig.description: Contains the configuration details of the Amazon S3 bucket for storing the results of the evaluation job.

lastModifiedTime.description: The time the evaluation job was last modified.

failureMessages.description: A list of strings that specify why the evaluation job failed to create.

inputModalities.description: The input modalities that the model supports.

outputModalities.description: The output modalities that the model supports.

inferenceTypesSupported.description: The inference types that the model supports.

modelLifecycle.description: Contains details about whether a model version is available or deprecated

status.description: Specifies whether a model version is available (ACTIVE) or deprecated (LEGACY.

modelId.description: The model Id of the foundation model.

status.description: Status of the agreement.
The status of the guardrail.

updatedAt.description: The date and time at which the guardrail was updated.

statusReasons.description: Appears if the status is FAILED. A list of reasons for why the guardrail failed to be created, updated, versioned, or deleted.

failureRecommendations.description: Appears if the status of the guardrail is FAILED. A list of recommendations to carry out before retrying the request.

blockedInputMessaging.description: The message that the guardrail returns when it blocks a prompt.

blockedOutputsMessaging.description: The message that the guardrail returns when it blocks a model response.

kmsKeyArn.description: The ARN of the KMS key that encrypts the guardrail.

createdAt.description: The time at which the inference profile was created.

updatedAt.description: The time at which the inference profile was last updated.

inferenceProfileArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the inference profile.

models.description: A list of information about each model in the inference profile.

inferenceProfileId.description: The unique identifier of the inference profile.

status.description: The status of the inference profile. ACTIVE means that the inference profile is ready to be used.

type.description:
The type of the inference profile. The following types are possible:

  • SYSTEM_DEFINED – The inference profile is defined by Amazon Bedrock. You can route inference requests across regions with these inference profiles.

  • APPLICATION – The inference profile was created by a user. This type of inference profile can track metrics and costs when invoking the model in it. The inference profile may route requests to one or multiple regions.

status.description: The status of the job. A successful job transitions from in-progress to completed when the output model is ready to use. If the job failed, the failure message contains information about why the job failed.

statusDetails.description: For a Distillation job, the details about the statuses of the sub-tasks of the customization job.

jobArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the batch inference job.

jobName.description: The name of the batch inference job.

modelId.description: The unique identifier of the foundation model used for model inference.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, case-sensitive identifier to ensure that the API request completes no more than one time. If this token matches a previous request, Amazon Bedrock ignores the request, but does not return an error. For more information, see Ensuring idempotency.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the service role with permissions to carry out and manage batch inference. You can use the console to create a default service role or follow the steps at Create a service role for batch inference.

status.description:
The status of the batch inference job.

The following statuses are possible:

  • Submitted – This job has been submitted to a queue for validation.

  • Validating – This job is being validated for the requirements described in Format and upload your batch inference data. The criteria include the following:

    • Your IAM service role has access to the Amazon S3 buckets containing your files.

    • Your files are .jsonl files and each individual record is a JSON object in the correct format. Note that validation doesn't check if the modelInput value matches the request body for the model.

    • Your files fulfill the requirements for file size and number of records. For more information, see Quotas for Amazon Bedrock.

  • Scheduled – This job has been validated and is now in a queue. The job will automatically start when it reaches its turn.

  • Expired – This job timed out because it was scheduled but didn't begin before the set timeout duration. Submit a new job request.

  • InProgress – This job has begun. You can start viewing the results in the output S3 location.

  • Completed – This job has successfully completed. View the output files in the output S3 location.

  • PartiallyCompleted – This job has partially completed. Not all of your records could be processed in time. View the output files in the output S3 location.

  • Failed – This job has failed. Check the failure message for any further details. For further assistance, reach out to the Amazon Web Services Support Center.

  • Stopped – This job was stopped by a user.

  • Stopping – This job is being stopped by a user.

message.description: If the batch inference job failed, this field contains a message describing why the job failed.

submitTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job was submitted.

lastModifiedTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job was last modified.

endTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job ended.

inputDataConfig.description: Details about the location of the input to the batch inference job.

outputDataConfig.description: Details about the location of the output of the batch inference job.

vpcConfig.description: The configuration of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) for the data in the batch inference job. For more information, see Protect batch inference jobs using a VPC.

timeoutDurationInHours.description: The number of hours after which batch inference job was set to time out.

jobExpirationTime.description: The time at which the batch inference job times or timed out.

s3InputDataConfig.description: Contains the configuration of the S3 location of the input data.

s3Uri.description: The S3 location of the input data.

s3BucketOwner.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account that owns the S3 bucket containing the input data.
